spec.reasoning: Lexical perturbation: Renamed all column headers across all three sheets consistently — ITEM→PRODUCT, ID→SKU, QTY→AMT on both source sheets, and MONTH1→PERIOD1, MONTH2→PERIOD2 on the merging sheet. Sheet names changed from SH1→JAN, RP1→FEB, merging→consolidated. These are all same-level descriptiveness renames (abbreviation to abbreviation, or short word to short word). The instruction references 'SH1' and 'RP1' and 'merging' by name, plus column names like 'ID', 'QTY', 'MONTH1', 'MONTH2' — renaming all of these tests whether the agent hardcodes these names. Distractor column 'UNIT' is added to both source sheets after the last column; it is plausibly related (units of measurement) but clearly different from AMT (quantity amount). The key column alignment is preserved: SKU↔SKU across all sheets. No data values needed renaming since header names don't appear as data values. No instruction paraphrase to avoid meaning drift.
